libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For renderer behavior, obtain SiteRenderer/DefaultSiteRenderer through the Plexus/Sisu container rather than plain new DefaultSiteRenderer(), because velocity, Doxia, parser module manager, and container fields are injected. For skin/template tests, create a temporary skin JAR containing META-INF/maven/site.vm and pass it through createContextForSkin. Update tests to the 2.0 API: RenderingContext and SkinResourceLoader are gone; use DocumentRenderingContext, SiteRenderingContext, SiteRendererSink, and the current Renderer/SiteRenderer methods.

deterministic_setup.reason: doxia-site-renderer exposes createContextForSkin(Artifact, ...) and upstream tests create skin artifacts with DefaultArtifact; maven-artifact is declared as provided, so Gradle consumer tests do not receive it transitively.
Doxia 2.0 parser modules are no longer all in doxia-core; APT rendering requires the module component descriptor on the test classpath.
XDoc rendering requires the separate parser module component descriptor on the test classpath.
FML rendering requires the separate parser module component descriptor on the test classpath.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8134 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.doxia:doxia-site-renderer:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The existing 1.9.1 tests use removed APIs, so a simple version bump will keep failing javac.

Parser-module dependencies are only needed if tests exercise real APT/XDoc/FML site rendering; a narrower test limited to XHTML5 or simple model/context APIs may compile without them but will provide weaker coverage.
Native-image verification may still expose resource/reflection requirements around Sisu, Velocity templates, or parser component descriptors.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Meaningful renderer coverage for doxia-site-renderer 2.0.0 usually needs Maven/Doxia runtime pieces that are not pulled by the normal direct library dependency: maven-artifact is a provided dependency for the public skin API, and non-XHTML Doxia parser modules are test-scoped in the artifact POM because parser component descriptors must be present on the classpath.
